text.title: PWA Core: Implementation Patterns
text.summary: Workbox service worker, registration module, and install prompt hook patterns.

rule.statement: Register the service worker once from a single bootstrap module (index.html or main.ts). Do not register inside React effects — StrictMode double-invokes effects in development, causing double registration.
rule.rationale: Double registration causes confusing lifecycle events and makes debugging SW updates harder.

section.title: Workbox Caching Strategies
section.reference: Full Workbox SW with all route strategies and offline fallback.

antipattern.description: LLMs hallucinate Workbox import names across versions (NetworkFirst vs networkFirst, CacheFirst vs cacheFirst).
antipattern.reason: Pin the Workbox major version and feed agent the exact import surface from the pinned package's types.

antipattern.description: SW code that reaches for window or document properties.
antipattern.reason: SW runs in ServiceWorkerGlobalScope, not window. Lint with eslint-plugin-workbox or a SW-specific tsconfig that excludes lib: ["dom"].

antipattern.description: beforeinstallprompt is captured inside a component that re-mounts.
antipattern.reason: The event fires once per session. Re-mounting loses the deferred prompt. Store it at module scope or in a singleton hook.

text.reference: Playwright smoke test for SW registration and offline navigation.
